requirement_text: Differential output voltage at MDI shall be between 0.67V and 1.33V peak-to-peak for 10GBASE-T operation
Output impedance at MDI shall be 85 to 115 Ohm differential for proper line matching
Return loss shall be at minimum 12 dB across the 1 to 500 MHz frequency range
Transmit pair skew shall not exceed 2.5 ns between any two pairs
Auto-negotiation shall be implemented and enabled by default on all 10/100/1000BASE-T interfaces
Clause 73 base page advertisement shall correctly encode all supported link modes
Link training and signal acquisition shall complete within 10 seconds of link partner detection
Energy Efficient Ethernet capability shall be advertised during auto-negotiation if implemented
All PCB traces carrying more than 1A continuous current shall have minimum width of 0.3mm at 25 degrees C ambient
Solder mask clearance from copper features shall be a minimum of 0.05mm on all sides
Via drill diameter shall not be less than 0.2mm for any signal or power layer connection
All components shall be rated for the specified operating temperature range of the assembly
Conformal coating shall be applied to assemblies designated for harsh or outdoor environments
Impedance controlled traces shall maintain specified impedance within 10 percent across the entire board
Each PCB assembly shall carry a unique serial number visible on the silkscreen layer
BGA component land patterns shall comply with IPC-7351 recommended footprint dimensions
Organisation shall determine external and internal issues relevant to its purpose and QMS
Organisation shall determine risks and opportunities that need to be addressed for QMS effectiveness
Organisation shall determine the necessary competence of persons doing work that affects quality
Organisation shall plan implement control and monitor processes needed to meet product requirements
Organisation shall establish and maintain a documented design and development process
Organisation shall monitor measure analyse and evaluate quality performance against planned results
Organisation shall react to nonconformities and take action to control and correct them
Listed or labelled equipment shall be installed and used per instructions included in the listing
Ground-fault circuit-interrupter protection shall be provided for personnel in specified locations
Service entrance conductors shall have an ampacity not less than the load to be served
Underground wiring shall be installed at minimum cover depths as specified per conductor type
Conductor ampacity shall be determined in accordance with the applicable table and installation conditions
Switchboards and panelboards shall be protected and accessible only to qualified persons
Wiring methods in Class I Division 1 locations shall be approved explosion-proof or intrinsically safe
All electrical installations shall be carried out by licensed contractors or under qualified engineer supervision
Earthing conductors shall have cross-sectional area not less than that specified in Table 3
All switchgear and controlgear shall be suitable for the prospective short-circuit current at point of installation
Cable routes shall be selected to minimise risks from mechanical damage heat fire water and corrosion
Every installation shall be protected against overcurrent by devices which interrupt the circuit before danger
Test certificates and inspection records shall be maintained for a minimum period of 10 years
All drawings shall use the inch or millimetre unit system consistently throughout with units stated in title block
Geometric tolerances shall be specified to control the form orientation location and runout of features
Datum reference frames shall be established using features that are functional to the assembly
Position tolerances shall be specified for all features that must be located relative to datum reference frames
Surface finish symbols shall be specified on all machined surfaces with roughness values in Ra or Rz
All dimensions shall have associated tolerances either directly stated in a general tolerance block or via geometric control
